title: Smith v3 Task Sub-agent Description Output
description: Strict JSON contract returned by a Task tool sub-agent (subagent_type=general, model=claude-haiku-4-5) invoked by `/smith-index --describe` or by the smith workflow incremental path. The sub-agent receives a description-generation prompt built by `describe_write.py build-prompt` and MUST return exactly one JSON object matching this schema (no preamble, no code fences, no commentary). The orchestrating LLM (skill prose) parses this payload, then pipes it into `describe_write.py` which translates the envelope into the on-disk `.meta` description layer (contract: meta-description-layer.schema.json — unchanged from PR #21).

status.description: Top-level status. `ok` means the sub-agent produced a usable description payload (which may still be partial — some method ids omitted). `error` means the sub-agent could not produce ANY usable output; the orchestrator records a `failed` JSONL entry and does NOT touch the `.meta`.

module_description.description: Module-level description, ≤120-char soft cap (400 hard cap to allow rare overflow; the writer truncates to soft cap). Single line, no newlines, no markdown formatting. Null when the sub-agent declined to produce a module description (e.g. file is data-only / no exported behavior) OR when status=error.

method_descriptions.description: Per-method descriptions. The array may be a subset of the prompt's `Methods to describe` list — methods absent from this array have no description for this Task. The writer drops stale ids automatically (mirrors v2 `_describe` stale-id cleanup) but does NOT invent descriptions for missing ids.

method_id.description: Stable 16-char hex method id from the parser output. MUST match an id present in the prompt's `Methods to describe` list — ids not in the prompt are silently discarded by the writer.

description.description: Per-method description, ≤200-char soft cap (400 hard cap). One-to-two sentences. No newlines, no markdown formatting. The writer applies `_truncate` (preserves natural sentence/word breaks) to enforce the soft cap.

errors.description: Optional list of error entries. Always present (may be empty). Used by status=ok responses to flag partial failures (e.g. one method id was too large for the sub-agent's budget) and by status=error responses to explain why no output was produced.

code.description: Machine-readable error tag. The orchestrator can use this to decide whether to retry, skip, or surface to the user.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation of the error. Logged to the JSONL record's `error` field on `failed` status.

method_id.description: Optional — when present, indicates this error applies only to the named method id (the rest of the response may still be usable).
